``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined By statute, fixed maximum terms must be included in most judicial sentences. Minimum terms can be as short as the judge elects but may not exceed two-thirds of the maximum sentence. There is no system of good-time earned-time or similar credits that may be accumulated by people in prison as a discount.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 27%, changing from 44,113 in 2010 to 32,186 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: Source: BJS Prisoners Series

# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2018, prison releases decreased 25%, dropping from 13,508 in 2010 to 10,095 in 2018. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
In 2018, 57% of people eligible for parole were released during their eligibility year. This represents a 8% decrease compared to 2010. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
